title: İsrail'in Beyrut'a hava saldırıları düzenlemek için ABD'den onay istediği iddia edildi
summary: İsrail'de yayın yapan Kanal 12 televizyonu, ismi açıklanmayan bir İsrailli yetkiliye dayandırdığı haberinde, İsrail Başbakanı Binyamin Netanyahu ile ABD Dışişleri Bakanı Marco Rubio arasında gerçekleştirilen telefon görüşmesinde Lübnan’a yönelik saldırıların ele alındığını öne sürdü. Habere göre Netanyahu, görüşmede Beyrut dahil olmak üzere Lübnan’a yönelik saldırıların genişletilmesi yönünde talepte bulundu. İsrailli yetkili, Washington yönetiminin İsrail’in bu talebine önceki dönemlere kıyasla daha “açık ve esnek” yaklaştığını iddia etti. İddiaya göre Netanyahu, Beyrut’un Hizbullah tarafından “korunaklı bölge” olarak görülmesinin Tel Aviv yönetimi açısından kabul edilemez olduğunu da Rubio’ya iletti. ABD ve İsrail makamları ise söz konusu telefon görüşmesinin içeriğine ilişkin herhangi bir resmi açıklama yapmadı. İsrail’den kademeli çekilme teklifi iddiası Öte yandan İsrail devlet televizyonu KAN’ın ABD’li kaynaklara dayandırdığı haberinde, Tel Aviv yönetiminin Hizbullah’ın silahsızlandırılması konusunda adım atılması şartıyla Lübnan’da işgal altında tuttuğu bölgelerden kademeli olarak çekilmeyi teklif etmeye hazırlandığı ileri sürüldü. Haberde, söz konusu teklifin 2-3 Haziran tarihlerinde Washington’da yapılması planlanan dördüncü tur Lübnan-İsrail görüşmelerinde gündeme getirilebileceği belirtildi. ABD’li kaynaklara göre teklif, Lübnan ordusunun Hizbullah’a yönelik operasyonlarını artırmasına paralel olarak İsrail ordusunun işgal ettiği bölgelerden aşamalı şekilde çekilmesini içeriyor. Ateşkese rağmen saldırılar sürüyor İsrail ordusunun, ABD Başkanı Donald Trump’ın baskısı nedeniyle Beyrut’a doğrudan hava saldırıları düzenleyemediği iddia edilirken, son olarak 28 Mayıs’ta ateşkese rağmen Beyrut’un güneyindeki Şuveyfet bölgesine hava saldırısı gerçekleştirdiği belirtildi. İsrail ordusu 2 Mart’ta Lübnan’a yönelik yoğun hava saldırıları başlatmış ve ülkenin güneyindeki birçok bölgeyi işgal etmişti. Lübnan hükümeti, saldırılar nedeniyle ülke içinde yerinden edilenlerin sayısının 1 milyonu aştığını açıklamıştı. ABD arabuluculuğunda yürütülen görüşmeler sonucunda 17 Nisan’da yürürlüğe giren geçici ateşkes daha sonra uzatılmış, 17 Mayıs itibarıyla ateşkesin 45 gün daha devam etmesi konusunda mutabakata varılmıştı. Lübnan Sağlık Bakanlığı’nın son verilerine göre, İsrail’in 2 Mart’tan bu yana düzenlediği saldırılarda hayatını kaybedenlerin sayısı 3 bin 371’e yükseldi. Netanyahu ise 25 Mayıs’ta yaptığı açıklamada, İsrail ordusuna Lübnan’a yönelik saldırıların artırılması talimatını verdiğini duyurmuştu. title: Aziz Yıldırım before the election: "We have the brains, the experience, and the money too"
summary: Aziz Yıldırım before the election: "We have the brains, the experience, and the money too" Fenerbahçe presidential candidate and former president Aziz Yıldırım gave a long pre-election interview covering football, transfers, finances, the stadium, the fan base, amateur branches, and the club's future. Here is the full breakdown. Summary Says the squad is good and only needs reinforcements; claims FB would have been champions this season if signings had been made Targeting 2 strikers (agreed in principle, no names given) plus at least 2 center-backs (one left-footed) Admits he does not know the club's exact debt and speaks only from "what I've heard"; cites a rumored 600M total / 193M due within a year UEFA: 60M spending limit; possible penalty over Kerem Aktürkoğlu's salary paid via "image rights" plus ongoing Mourinho compensation Almost every concrete question answered with "we'll look into it once elected" Heavy on past achievements and "unity/spirit" rhetoric, light on verifiable commitments "We'll play a European final" Yıldırım said he believes Fenerbahçe will one day play a Champions League final, and that wide squads are needed for it. He recalled reaching certain stages in the past in UEFA competitions and the Champions League, and pointed to bad luck in the Benfica tie specifically (three suspended players, Gökhan Gönül being injured and hospitalized), which he said cost a potential final against Chelsea. He also laid out this season's European roadmap: the early rounds feature relatively weak opponents (such as Gornik Zabrze of Poland), which can be passed comfortably if the squad is built by June 20-25; through the playoff stage the club could reach the Champions League for the first time, and this level should then be made permanent in following years. "We'd have been champions this season if we'd reinforced" Yıldırım said that, just as they stopped Galatasaray's back-to-back titles in 2000-2001, they can do the same this season, and that he became a candidate because he believes this, adding he would not have run otherwise. He said the team is not bad but quality, and the only issue is that the gaps were not filled. "If we had made the reinforcements this season, we would already have been champions," he said, arguing that without the 2-0 loss at Karagümrük and home dropped points against teams like Alanya/Rize, FB would have finished 6-7 points ahead of the rival rather than behind. He told fans to take their flags out of the drawer and start preparing. Coach: "It won't necessarily be Aykut Kocaman" Yıldırım said the coaching matter has not yet been discussed within the board, that Aykut Kocaman is one of the candidates, and that talks are also being held with three foreign names. He defended Kocaman at length: scoring goals that brought the title from Trabzon in 1995-96, coming from 9 points behind at the first-half mark in 2011-12 to take 17 wins in 18 second-half matches, leading the club during the period he himself was imprisoned, constantly developing himself, and being a straightforward character. He said Kocaman's only flaw is not building relationships with the press and social media. Still, he said "we don't have the idea that it must be him; the facts must be laid out." On the financial side, he said Kocaman wants a 15M salary and 5M bonus, that Fenerbahçe's resources are not abundant, but that if a title were guaranteed the money could be paid. Using the Mourinho example, he said he wants a coach who works with the board and whose mistakes can be pointed out, rather than one too valuable to be challenged. On naming a coach, he closed the topic by saying "the name isn't important; the right transfer and the right coach are." Transfers: two strikers, two center-backs Yıldırım said two strikers have been agreed in principle but he won't announce them before deals with their clubs are done, otherwise prices would rise and a Mourinho-like situation could occur. He said one player wanted to come on Monday but was asked to wait until the club agreements are complete. He said the announcement could become clear Tuesday-Wednesday and could be made before the election if the board wishes. He did not confirm the Vedat Muriç and Sehrou Guirassy names that were raised, saying "there are many names on the list, let's be patient a few days." He said at least two center-backs will be signed, one of them left-footed, and that the need for a right-back may also arise. On Sıddık Şerif, he said the player could be good in the future but that paying a 22M fee for an urgently needed position is too much, and that with 20M more, a good striker could be bought for 40M. He said there are 19 foreign players in the squad and that the 10+4 rule (4 of them under 23) creates difficulty. He said three players (Nene, Brown, Şerif) meet the young quota and the others must fit into the 10-man list. He said players including Diego Carlos are being reviewed via their matches, and decisions on who stays or goes will be made after being elected and sitting down to talk, that speaking from memory now would be wrong. He said he will no longer pay departing players, and that an underperforming player will be sold directly rather than even loaned out. On goalkeeper Ederson specifically, he said everyone blames the keeper but the real problem is the defense, that because the defense pushes above the 18-yard line, Ederson cannot play as he did at Manchester City, and that both the keeper and the man in front of him are at fault. He said the money owed to Ederson so far has been paid and that he "heard" around 11M more will be paid, and that the Brazilian player should be won over and kept. He had a lengthy tactical discussion on back-four and three-man defense setups with Şenol Çorlu and Abdullah Ercan; on Oğuz Aydın he said "he has talent but has regressed, and should be developed with individual work and motivation." UEFA spending limit and penalty risk Yıldırım said he spoke with UEFA and there is a 60M euro spending limit, but that this figure will be reassessed after the licensing application once the financial statements are seen. He said money from player sales and sponsorship increases will be added to the limit, but that excessively raising sponsorship revenue (for example from 20 to 50M) could trigger a UEFA investigation. On penalty risk he raised the Kerem Aktürkoğlu example: the club pays the transfer fee, but the salaries are paid by current president Hakan Bey under the name "image rights," which UEFA wants paid through the club. He said if the payment is made to the club and then from the club to the player, no penalty would come. He also said that after Mourinho's departure, compensation is still being paid monthly, and that this and similar payments should be deducted from the UEFA limit, which he "heard." He said the penalty could be first a fine, then points if it escalates, and in the worst case exclusion from European competitions. Finances: "I don't know the exact figures" On debt figures, Yıldırım repeatedly said he is not fully informed and speaks only from what he has heard from public discussion. He relayed the rumor of a 600M euro total debt, saying "they say it's not that much; there's apparently around 193M euro due within a year." He said "the situation isn't good, we'll come in accordingly, but we won't blame the past," that if a wrongdoing is found the necessary action will be taken, but that speaking now would be wrong. He recalled that when he first came to Fenerbahçe the budget was 13M, which they raised to 250M by 2008, with the company value reaching 1.8 billion. He said revenues will be increased (stadium, boxes, sponsorship, jersey sales) and that he does not fear these. He said sponsors will come on their own once a title arrives. He highlighted two concrete projects: a real-estate project (worth around 60-70M) and a new commercial company to be founded. He said that with determination, 150-200M could enter the club at once after the election. He said the financial gap with Galatasaray is at most 50-100M and that this can be covered, that both teams scored 77 goals last season but Fenerbahçe conceded 37 to Galatasaray's 30, the problem being in defense. He said Galatasaray is also spending its real-estate revenues (like Florya), which is natural. Stadium: 64,000 capacity Yıldırım explained that the project to raise the stadium to 64,000 capacity is being run by Nihat Özdemir's team. He detailed that from behind the two goals, without touching the existing facilities, piles would be driven 5-10 meters out, concrete up to 2 meters with steel above, and by raising the roofs about 3 meters, seats would be added inward and behind the Maraton/Fener stands, bringing capacity to 64,000. He said the most critical point is obtaining permits from the sports authority and the municipality; if permits are secured by May, it will be done within that period, otherwise it spills into summer. He said the same method is being used at Barcelona and that Nihat Özdemir personally said "I'll do it." On the E-tribün complaints, he said it is not empty but looks empty in the broadcast because the seats are wide; still, he said he will review the revenue-cost analysis and could restore it to its old state if needed. On the Samandıra facilities, he said he doesn't know the situation and will inspect it once elected, and regarding Tedesco's complaint about training footage being filmed, he said this has happened for years and no coach has complained. Maltepe project Yıldırım said he himself secured the acquisition of the Maltepe land from the military. He recounted meeting then-Defense Minister Hulusi Akar and afterward "Yaşar Paşa" in 2018, that Murat Kurum also directed him, and that it was allocated from the National Real Estate office via petition. He said they planned to build the university's main building and sports facilities there, that he doesn't know the content of the current project, and that once elected he will inspect it and proceed if it is beneficial to Fenerbahçe and a fundable project. Stands and fans: 29 groups will become one voice Yıldırım said roughly 29 separate fan groups have formed in the stands, each saying different things, and that he will unify them into a single voice. He said the membership has risen above 60,000 (about 44,000 eligible to vote in this election), that the stands have gotten younger but the old strong "Kadıköy pressure" has weakened, and that he will recreate this atmosphere. He said he will bring young fans who stopped attending matches out of sadness back to the stands and make them feel comfortable walking the streets. He said he did not allow chants or flares during the campaign, finding them unnecessary, that they are candidates for a sports club presidency not politicians, and that flares hit people's heads and smell bad. He said the opposing side is also Fenerbahçe supporters and that love can be shown by shaking hands or nodding. Inside the club: "Club culture is gone" Yıldırım said the unifying role of the late Serkan Acar in the club has not been filled, that former athletes who come to the club are no longer welcomed, that those going to watch training are not let in and are offended. He said the people who form the club's mind were purged, and that club culture and corporatization were confused. He said he will open Dereağzı and the places former athletes used to gather to everyone, returning to a club-culture order where families can bring their children and athletes can spend time. Amateur branches He said Ömer Onan will be brought in as a board member in the football branch and will also oversee basketball. He said a name will also be appointed for volleyball but has not yet been decided. He said board and outside members will be appointed to the other amateur branches, and that if needed, branches could again be handed to strong brands or committees as in the past. In basketball, he said he is happy with Šarūnas Jasikevičius, that Obradović recommended this coach to him upon leaving. He said the current budget is around 29M and the deficit around 1M, which can be covered comfortably, and that spending can be made for better transfers. He said Fenerbahçe men's basketball should always be in the Final Four, that the title can vary year to year, but Fenerbahçe should be in the final. Referees and federation Yıldırım said there is a problem with referees in Turkey, that in the past they requested young referees but those too drifted into the same behavior over time. He said referees should not reflect their outside anger onto the pitch. He said if elected they will sit down and talk with the TFF and the Central Referee Committee, that they come for solutions not conflict, that their only demand is that their rights not be denied and that matches be governed fairly, otherwise they will set their stance accordingly. He emphasized the importance of the phrase "Turkey needs Fenerbahçe, Fenerbahçe needs Turkey." He said the 10+4 foreign rule could cause difficulty and that they will speak with the federation if needed. He said the biggest problem is that the Clubs Union does not function, that taxes have risen to around 40%, that in 2-3 years no one will be able to pay taxes, that broadcast revenues have fallen (they raised it from 160M dollars to 450M dollars in their time), and that all clubs' problems should be gathered and taken to Ankara together. He said having city teams in the league is positive for raising the brand value. Five-year vision and 100-day plan Yıldırım said they will prepare not a single-season but a five-year vision, to be announced to the public as the items are completed. The vision's headings: a sustainable success model in Europe, new revenue sources, digital transformation, reorganization of infrastructure and facilities, a "world brand Fenerbahçe" goal, and a financial discipline plan. For the 100-day short-term solution package, he listed the reorganization of the football structure, the establishment/development of a scouting system, a financial discipline plan, infrastructure integration (merging the youth setup with the senior team), and sponsorship and revenue-increasing moves. However, he deferred the details, saying "let's announce it if we win the election, let's not work for nothing." Management: "I'm coming to lead" Yıldırım said he is coming for leadership, not the presidency, that he has no personal presidential expectation, that he would gain more respect staying outside, but that he ran to stop Fenerbahçe's 12-year title drought. He emphasized that his call for unity remains valid even after the election. He said the new board is a mix of experience and youth, that it contains names who could in the future become president at Fenerbahçe or in Turkish football, and that he wants them to face the public instead of himself. He highlighted a valuable figure named Feyzun Bey, joining from Ankara, who will be brought into the football branch since he is not a Fenerbahçe member, and who did football work at Ankaragücü. He said they will form a "football brain" with former athletes and these executives. The past, "the organization," and election timing Yıldırım said he will investigate members from other clubs who became members to benefit from the club's values, and those tied to "the organization," that he will issue a call and sever ties with those who do not resign. He recounted the 3 July process at length: that it was the work not just against Fenerbahçe but of an organization trying to seize Turkey, that each group was tried separately in cases like Ergenekon, Balyoz, and OdaTV, that in the match-fixing case there were no players, referees, or officials from the opposing team, that the MASAK report showed no money entering or leaving, and that they were accused of fixing matches against themselves. He said the company value, which had reached 1.8 billion when a public offering was being planned, fell to 300 million with 3 July. On the September election timing, he said he advised Ali Koç to hold the election in June, that he said it would be wrong, but that Ali Koç's team went to Dubai and the process shifted to September after the basketball title. He said there would have been no problem if held in June, and that the Benfica elimination, Mourinho's pressure for players, and late transfers brought the process to this point. On family At the end of the program, on a question about his daughter, he relayed that when asked at a charity association in London "are you thinking of becoming president?", she answered "I am, but I don't know what life will bring." He also said his daughter will not make any broadcasts about Fenerbahçe in the final week before the election, because it harms both her and the campaign. Yıldırım summarized his message with the words "the brains are ours, the experience is ours, we're putting up the money too, what else should we do," reiterating that they focus not on settling scores with the past but on solutions and the future. Source: A Spor, 30 May 2026 submitted by /u/Ok-Property-5895 to r/FenerbahceSK link yorumlar

title: CHP’li İlhan Kesici: Bölünme endişesi artıyor, en büyük sorumluluk Kılıçdaroğlu’nda
summary: CHP İstanbul Milletvekili İlhan Kesici, Ankara’da aynı saatlerde yapılan iki ayrı CHP toplantısına ilişkin değerlendirmelerde bulundu. Kesici, söz konusu görüntünün parti içinde ve kamuoyunda “bölünme endişesi” oluşturduğunu söyledi. Ankara’da CHP Genel Merkezi önünde ve Güvenpark’ta düzenlenen iki farklı toplantıya dikkat çeken Kesici, her iki organizasyonun da kendi tabanını tatmin eden kalabalıklara sahne olduğunu ifade etti. Kesici açıklamasında, “Dün Ankara’da tam aynı saatlerde iki büyük CHP toplantısı yapıldı. Bunlardan biri CHP Genel Merkezi önünde Sayın Kemal Kılıçdaroğlu’nun yaptığı toplantıydı. Diğeri ise Sayın Özgür Özel’in 5 kilometre uzaklıktaki Güvenpark’ta yaptığı toplantıydı” dedi. “En büyük sorumluluk Kılıçdaroğlu’nda” Bu tablonun parti içinde “acaba bölünme mi yaşanıyor” sorusunu gündeme getirdiğini belirten Kesici, şunları söyledi: “Eğer böyle bir endişe varsa, bunun önüne geçmek için bıkmadan usanmadan çaba gösterilmelidir. Bu aşamada en büyük sorumluluk, hukuken genel başkan olan Sayın Kemal Kılıçdaroğlu’ndadır. Elbette Sayın Özgür Özel ve arkadaşları da aynı yönde gayret göstermelidir.” Tarihsel örnekler: Bölünmeler iyi sonuç vermedi Kesici, geçmişte yaşanan siyasi ayrışmalara da değinerek Adalet Partisi ve Demokratik Parti sürecini örnek gösterdi. Bu ayrışmanın Türk siyasi tarihinde önemli bir kırılma olduğunu söyleyen Kesici, dönemin etkilerinin uzun vadeli sonuçlar doğurduğunu ifade etti. Ayrıca dönemin siyasi aktörlerinden Süleyman Demirel’in ilerleyen yıllarda bu süreci “siyasi hayatının en büyük hatalarından biri” olarak değerlendirdiğini hatırlattı. “Birlik ve beraberlik en doğru seçenek” Açıklamasının sonunda Kesici, CHP içinde sağduyu çağrısı yaparak, “Zor olan ama en doğru olan yol bir ve beraber olmaktır. Bugün gösterilecek her çaba, hem parti hem de ülke siyaseti açısından değerli olacaktır” dedi. Independent Türkçe ilhan kesici CHP CHP İstanbul Milletvekili İlhan Kesici, Ankara’da aynı saatlerde düzenlenen iki ayrı CHP toplantısının partide “bölünme endişesini artırdığını” belirterek, “En büyük sorumluluk Kılıçdaroğlu’ndadır” dedi Pazar, Mayıs 31, 2026 - 23:15 Main image: Fotoğraf: ANKA Siyaset Type: news SEO Title: CHP’li İlhan Kesici: Bölünme endişesi artıyor, en büyük sorumluluk Kılıçdaroğlu’nda copyright Independentturkish:

title: İsrail hapishanelerindeki idari tutuklu sayısı mayıs ayı itibarıyla 3 bin 376'ya yükseldi
summary: Filistin Esirler Cemiyeti ile Filistin Esirler ve Serbest Bırakılanlar Heyeti konuya ilişkin ortak yazılı açıklama yaptı. İsrail hapishanelerindeki 139 esir hakkında 2 ila 6 ay arasında değişen idari tutukluluk kararı alındığı kaydedildi. Bunlardan bir kısmının yeni kararlar, bir kısmının ise daha önceki idari tutuklulukların yenilenmesi şeklinde olduğu ifade edildi. İsrail yönetiminin, "gizli bir dosyanın varlığını" bahane ederek idari tutukluluk uygulamasını sıklıkla kullandığı belirtilen açıklamada, hapishanelerdeki idari tutuklu sayısının mayıs ayı itibarıyla 3 bin 376'ya ulaştığı aktarıldı. İsrail, "idari tutukluluk" adını verdiği uygulamayla Filistinlileri 1 aydan 6 aya kadar alıkoyabiliyor. AA İsrail Filistin İsrail yönetiminin, hapishanelerdeki Filistinli esirlerden 139'u hakkında "idari tutukluluk" kararı aldığı belirtildi. summary: Dokuz günlük Kurban Bayramı tatilinin son gününde, memleketlerinden dönen yurttaşlar yollara döküldü. Türkiye’nin doğu ve kuzeyindeki 43 ilin geçiş güzergahı olan ‘kilit kavşak’ Kırıkkale’de kilometrelerce araç kuyruğu oluşurken, yoğunluğun gece geç saatlere kadar sürmesi bekleniyor.

title: 1'i bebek 8 kişinin öldüğü kazada yeni ayrıntılar ortaya çıktı
summary: Denizli'nin Sarayköy ilçesinde bariyerlere çarptıktan sonra yanan yolcu otobüsünde 1'i bebek 8 kişinin hayatını kaybettiği, 33 kişinin yaralandığı kazada, yaralıların ilk ifadeleri ortaya çıktı. Yaralıların, jandarmadaki ifadelerinde, kazadan yaklaşık 20 dakika önce şoförün araçta meydana gelen bir arıza nedeniyle otobüsü yol kenarına çekip kontrol yaptığını, daha sonra yola devam ettiğini söyledikleri öğrenildi.
